subject: LU-7919 mount: Buffer overflow issue while parsing mount

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

(7 comments)

Patch Set 6:

> (defect) this is not necessarily an error, since some of the  
 > Lustre mount options are not passed to the kernel, but only  
 > processed internally (e.g. "retry=N" and 
 > "md_stripe_cache_size=N") which may mean that the resulting 
 > mount option string is shorter than g_pagesize.
 > This check should just be removed and let parse_options()  
 > decide if the buffer is overflowed.


Removing this error will cause buffer-overflow because we are copying mop.orig_options to options and then pass it to parse_options

https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/bb6c7ec6-5de3-11e6-906c-5254006e85c2

My test case is looking for the error string when mount options exceeds the size limit , In our case it is checking for "mount options too long" as test was failing consistently I have updated patch with on debug statement 

It is  observed that on maloo test-board I am getting this error "mcmd: Bad read of expected verification number off of stderr socket: Success Start of /dev/lvm-Role_MDS/P1 on mds1 failed 254"  which is unexpected. I have put detailed logs and comparison on JIRA ticket.

parents.subject: LU-8630 build: Fix definition of KMP_MODDIR

commit.subject: LU-7919 mount: Buffer overflow issue while parsing mount
commit.message:
LU-7919 mount: Buffer overflow issue while parsing mount

check is added in parse_options, parse_ldd, add_mgsnids,
parse_ldd and main function to avoid buffer overflow
while parsing mount options.

Seagate-bug-id: MRP-2384
